description: Router-programmed stage binding vectors (docs/SUBLOCATION_ADVERTISEMENT.md). Cross-platform lockstep across loxation-sw (iOS honor), loxation-android (honor mirror), and blemesh-router (emitter) — check the same file into all three repos (fixtures/README.md convention). Groups: 'stageHash' + 'encode' are normative for the router emitter; 'parse' + 'effectiveMapping' are normative for the phone clients. The advertised overlay is TIER-2 binding only: the pack manifest stays authoritative for venue MEMBERSHIP (memberBeaconIds — keep-alive/repromotion are hash-blind) and stage VALIDITY (declared sublocations); anything less than an unambiguously valid advertisement is treated as ABSENT (fall back to the static tier-1 binding / membership-only), NEVER as a demotion. Nothing is persisted client-side — the overlay is recomputed per flush from fresh sightings.

stageHash.rule: stageHash = first 4 bytes of SHA256(UTF-8(stageId)), compared BYTEWISE (never as an integer). stageId is the exact declared sublocation id string — already constrained to lowercase [a-z0-9-]{1,64}, so UTF-8 == ASCII; the router validates (trim, lowercase, regex) BEFORE hashing. No trailing NUL/newline.

vectors.note: collision pair with collide-c21f — pre-brute-forced (4-byte prefixes collide in seconds); used by effectiveMapping ambiguity vector

encode.rule: Router emitter. androidPayloadHex = the bytes passed to addManufacturerData(0xFFFF, payload); iosMdHex = the manufacturer data as CoreBluetooth surfaces it (company id prepended little-endian; 0xFFFF is byte-order-symmetric). NO stage configured -> legacy layout [uuid:16]. Stage configured -> v1 layout [ver=0x01][stageHash:4][uuid:16]. beacon_uuid is the TRAILING 16 bytes in every version, forever (MUST — this is what keeps every deployed client's suffix-16 membership extraction correct). Version 0x00 is reserved-invalid and never emitted.

vectors.name: legacy-uuid-first-byte-0x01
vectors.note: md[2]==0x01 looks like a version byte but length says legacy — pairs with the parse trap vector

parse.rule: Phone clients, generic (non-Apple) manufacturer-data branch. LENGTH GATES BEFORE VERSION: md.count==18 -> legacy, uuid=suffix(16), no hash. md.count==23 AND md[0..1]==ffff AND md[2]==0x01 -> uuid=suffix(16), stageHash=md[3..6]. md.count==23 with any other version, any other length >=16, or a non-ffff company -> uuid=suffix(16), NO hash (pre-existing behavior verbatim). Apple prefix (4c00) -> no mfr-derived id at all (never a stage sighting). len<16 -> whole-md hex string id (pre-existing behavior), never a hash. expect.beaconId is the NORMALIZED sighting key (lowercase, dash-strip).

vectors.name: trap-legacy-18B-with-md2-0x01
vectors.note: length gates before version — a legacy payload whose uuid[0]==0x01 MUST parse as legacy

vectors.name: apple-prefix-no-mfr-id
vectors.note: Apple company id -> extractGlobalBeaconId nil -> never a stage sighting (CB-peripheral fallback ids can't appear in a manifest)

effectiveMapping.rule: effective = manifest.stageMapping (post-parse tier-1 statics); then for each FRESH sighting carrying a stageHash: require sighting.beaconId IN memberBeaconIds (else ignore entirely), require the hash to match EXACTLY ONE declared sublocation id (0 or >=2 matches -> advertisement treated as absent), then effective[beaconId] = matchedStageId (tier-2 override wins while the sighting is fresh). Overlay lifetime == sighting lifetime (25s fresh window); when the advertisement disappears the static binding resumes at the next flush. Selector dynamics (margin/confirm-flush/TTL) run downstream of this map and are NOT fixture-driven (per-platform unit tests, existing convention). rssi values are irrelevant to the mapping itself.
effectiveMapping.manifestNote: Post-parse statics: stageMapping = {a1b2..: mainstage, 0102.._11_22: bassriver} (the 'ghost' binding parse-demotes to membership-only; 0bad../c001.. are membership-only spares). memberBeaconIds = all five keys.

vectors.name: collision-hash-ambiguous-treated-absent
vectors.note: 6a35cc01 matches BOTH collide-7912 and collide-c21f -> ambiguous -> absent: the spare stays membership-only, the static binding survives

vectors.name: hashless-sighting-falls-back-to-static
vectors.note: same shape as the previous vector minus the hash — the override evaporates with the advertisement, no hold-down timer

vectors.name: n-routers-one-stage
vectors.note: N:1 is first-class — three beacons now bind mainstage; the selector downstream takes the strongest RSSI per stage as it always has

vectors.name: declared-stage-with-no-static-binding-binds
vectors.note: solardome is declared with ZERO static bindings — a venue may author stages and bind every router on-site at deploy time

vectors.name: parse-demoted-ghost-with-valid-adv-binds
vectors.note: the static 'ghost' binding demoted at parse (undeclared stage); a valid live advertisement still binds this member beacon
